question: Does the generated text outline a structural plan for a 12-page zine layout as requested?
Does the response include exactly 4 generated images representing the layout mockups for the selected spreads?
Does the response include generated images for both the Front Cover and the Back Cover?
Do the photographic elements within the generated mockups clearly depict the theme 'Industrial Ruins on the Urban Fringe' (e.g., rusted metal, abandoned factories, overgrown vegetation)?
Do the generated spread mockups clearly depict a two-page book layout (e.g., visible center gutter or side-by-side page composition) rather than single isolated pages?
Do the generated layout mockups visually demonstrate the requested 'Japanese-style photobook aesthetic' with generous white space (negative space)?
Do the 4 generated spread mockups demonstrate distinct layout rhythms (e.g., varying between full-bleed images, grid arrangements, and text-heavy pages) rather than repeating the same template?
Do the generated layout mockups show clear typographic hierarchy (e.g., distinguishing between titles, captions, and body text) consistent with the request for 'distinctive typography'?
Does the text description for each spread accurately match the visual layout shown in the adjacent generated image (e.g., if text says 'full-bleed image on left', the image shows that)?
Do all generated images (spreads and covers) maintain a consistent visual style (color palette, lighting, typographic treatment) creating a cohesive book identity?
Do the generated images serve as informative layout mockups (showing the book object, page boundaries, or typographic placement) rather than just being raw, unformatted photographs?
Are the generated images placed immediately adjacent to the textual description of that specific spread or cover?
